title: Isolated ring cavity resonator
abstract: Described herein are isolated ring cavities that have refractive and heat-generating components physically separated and mechanically held by flexure mounts that are adapted to function in combination with the physically separated structure to moderate the thermal expansion effects of the heat generated by the refractive and other heat-generating elements (e.g., gain element) of the optical cavity. The flexure mounts may be configured as thinned portions of connective elements, reducing the effects of thermal expansion of the baseplate and allowing a thermal isolation from the baseplate. Multiple flexure mounts may be arranged to minimize further the effects of thermal expansion of the baseplate.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: an optical cavity with an optical path formed of a plurality of bulk components; a first portion of the resonator cavity defining a first portion of the optical path with a primary optical plane that includes at least two consecutive legs of the optical path created by a first portion of the plurality of bulk elements; a first rigid structure holding the first portion of the plurality of bulk components in relative position to form the first portion of the optical path; a secondary portion of the resonator cavity defining a refractive portion of the optical path that includes the path traversing a refractive element of the cavity capable of producing gain in the optical path; a second rigid structure including a heatsink supporting the refractive element that contains the refractive portion of the optical path; and at least one flexible support connecting the first rigid structure from the second rigid structure in relative position to the refractive element in order to create the optical path including the refractive portion of the optical path. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the second rigid structure only supports the refractive element and supports none other of the plurality of bulk components forming the optical path. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the at least one flexible support and the second rigid structure are further adapted to hold the primary optical plane of the optical cavity in a position to maintain the optical path of the optical cavity while the refractive element generates two different heat outputs caused by two different levels of power delivered to the refractive element. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the at least one flexible support is further adapted to hold the primary optical plane of the optical cavity in a position that the refractive element is adapted to transfer heat generated within the refractive element to the baseplate via a heat transfer within the primary optical plane. 5. The system of claim 1, wherein the at least one flexible support is further adapted to hold 

 the baseplate in a position receive the heat generated from the refractive element within the primary optical plane. 6. The system of claim 1, wherein the baseplate is further adapted to spread any heat received from the refractive element into a baseplate reference plane that is orthogonal to the primary optical plane of the cavity.
